articles.title: Does the Location of the Distal Tibial Epiphyseal Fixation Affect Rod Failure in Children With Osteogenesis Imperfecta?
articles.authors: Wang K, Callister J, Wallace M
articles.population: Children with osteogenesis imperfecta undergoing tibial fixation
articles.outcomes: Failure to telescope (primary outcome) and time to failure (secondary outcome)
articles.take_home: Central positioning of Fassier-Duval rods had the lowest failure rate, whilst lateral positioning significantly increased the risk of failure. Younger children were more likely to experience rod failure.

articles.title: Ideal Surgical Age for Distraction-Based Growth Friendly Techniques to Minimize Complications.
articles.authors: Beydemir A, Ibik S, Ramazanov R, Berktas M, Ayvaz M, Demirkiran HG, et al.
articles.population: Children with early-onset scoliosis treated with growing rods
articles.outcomes: Complication rates and severity, need for reoperation, comparison between age groups (85 months or younger versus older) and rod types (traditional versus magnetically controlled)
articles.take_home: Children having growing rod surgery before 85 months of age experienced more overall complications than those aged 85 months or older, but the complications were not more severe. Delaying surgery until 85 months or later may help reduce problems when it is safe to do so.

articles.title: Minimal Clinically Important Difference (MCID) of the EOSQ-24 Questionnaire.
articles.authors: Park AM, Elfilali MM, Brown MW, Taha O, Weintraub MJ, Garg S, et al.
articles.study_type: Prospective multicentre study to determine minimal clinically important difference
articles.population: Children with early onset scoliosis and/or their parents
articles.outcomes: Minimal clinically important difference (MCID) for EOSQ-24 questionnaire scores measuring health-related quality of life
articles.take_home: A change of 5 points or more on the EOSQ-24 questionnaire represents a meaningful difference in quality of life for children with early onset scoliosis. This gives doctors a reliable way to measure whether treatment has made a real difference to patients beyond just looking at spine X-rays.

articles.title: Tether Breakage Across First-generation and Second-generation Vertebral Body Tethering Devices in Treating Adolescent Idiopathic Scoliosis.
articles.authors: Coleman TA, Kim A, Desai V, Parent S, Miyanji F, Alanay A, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective analysis of prospectively collected multicenter registry
articles.population: Patients with adolescent idiopathic scoliosis treated with vertebral body tethering
articles.outcomes: Rate of radiographic tether breakage, revision surgery, conversion to posterior spinal fusion
articles.take_home: Tether breakage occurred in about half of patients overall and remained similarly common with newer devices as with older ones, despite surgeons reporting fewer breakages with the newer systems. The rate of patients needing revision surgery or conversion to fusion was low and similar between the two generations.

articles.title: Radiographic Measurement of Lower Limb Length Discrepancy: A Comparison of Three Measurement Techniques for Assessing Total and Segmental Limb Lengths.
articles.authors: Kadhim M, Kelly N, Sabharwal S
articles.population: Skeletally immature patients with limb length discrepancy scheduled for epiphysiodesis
articles.outcomes: Comparison of three radiographic measurement methods for total and segmental limb length; measurement agreement between methods
articles.take_home: All three radiographic methods for measuring limb length difference gave very similar results, with differences of less than 2 millimetres between methods. Using the same measurement method consistently is important, especially when patients have curved or angled legs, as this can affect how accurate the measurements are.

articles.title: Animal-Assisted Therapy Reduces Patient Reported Pain During Office Pin Removal Following Supracondylar Humerus Fracture Fixation: A Randomized Controlled Study.
articles.authors: Arif HA, Feijoo E, Padgett M, Nishikawa A, Williams KA, Strom SF, et al.
articles.population: Children aged 3 to 10 years undergoing pin removal after supracondylar humerus fracture fixation
articles.outcomes: Patient-reported pain using Wong-Baker FACES scale, behavioural distress using FLACC scale, heart rate, and parental-reported anxiety
articles.take_home: Children who had a certified therapy dog present during pin removal reported significantly lower pain and behavioural distress compared to those without a dog, suggesting animal-assisted therapy could help reduce discomfort during this procedure.

articles.title: Naviculectomy combined with 'a la carte' soft-tissue releases: is it a practical solution for congenital vertical talus in adolescence?
articles.authors: Khamies MS, Mortada-Mahmoud A, Laklouk M
articles.population: adolescent patients with neglected congenital vertical talus
articles.sample_size: 15 patients (11 boys, 4 girls), average age 10.67 years
articles.outcomes: clinical function using Zorer score, pain using visual analogue scale, and radiographic measurements
articles.take_home: Removing the navicular bone combined with targeted soft-tissue releases improved foot function, reduced pain and radiographic deformity in adolescents with neglected congenital vertical talus, whilst potentially reducing complications from more extensive surgery.

articles.title: A mini-review of risk factors for developmental dysplasia of the hip: insights from multiple comparative cohort studies.
articles.authors: Cho T, Nair A, Stokey P, Kaufmann K, Liu J
articles.population: Infants at risk of developmental dysplasia of the hip
articles.outcomes: Relative risk of developmental dysplasia of the hip associated with breech presentation, family history, oligohydramnios, and female sex
articles.take_home: Babies born in breech position, those with a family history of hip dysplasia, those exposed to low amniotic fluid during pregnancy, and female babies all have three to four times higher risk of developing hip dysplasia. These findings support screening infants with these risk factors.

articles.title: Safety and efficacy of intraoperative vectored cranial traction with Gardner-Wells tongs for pediatric spine deformity surgery.
articles.authors: Sardesai KS, Georger L, Dumas A, Noe M, Schwend RM
articles.population: Children aged 2-18 years undergoing spine deformity surgery in prone position
articles.outcomes: Pressure-related complications including facial abrasions, pressure sores, ulcers and facial swelling; perioperative vision loss
articles.take_home: Using Gardner-Wells tongs with gentle traction during spine surgery reduced pressure-related complications to 1.42%, with no cases of vision loss. All complications that did occur were minor and reversible.

articles.title: Treatment and outcomes of fractures in children: a registry-based cohort study of 10,144 fractures.
articles.authors: Laaksonen T, Grahn P, Ahonen M, Ahola JA, Helenius I, Nietosvaara Y
articles.population: Children with fractures treated at a Level I trauma centre in Helsinki
articles.outcomes: Surgical timing, treatment method, complication rates, unplanned return to surgery, permanent nerve injuries, and deep infections
articles.take_home: Most children's fractures were treated safely with low complication rates. Surgery for serious arm and leg fractures happened quickly, and serious complications like nerve damage or deep infections were very rare.

articles.title: Postpartum haemorrhage: epidemiology, consequences, and missed opportunities.
articles.authors: Coomarasamy A, Sindhu KN, Gallos I, Periyathambi N, Price MJ, Yunas I, et al.
articles.population: Women giving birth worldwide
articles.sample_size: Approximately 27 million women with postpartum haemorrhage annually
articles.outcomes: Mortality (43,000 deaths annually), prevalence (12.6% after vaginal birth, 30.9% after caesarean birth), morbidity (severe anaemia, hysterectomy, organ failure, psychological trauma), economic burden (US$10.4 billion annually)
articles.take_home: Postpartum haemorrhage remains a leading cause of maternal death worldwide, occurring in millions of women each year with serious health and economic consequences. The World Health Organisation has updated how postpartum haemorrhage is defined and recommends comprehensive prevention, early diagnosis and prompt treatment to save lives.

articles.title: Prevention of postpartum haemorrhage: from evidence to implementation at scale.
articles.authors: Gallos ID, Sindhu KN, Yunas I, Sobhy S, Mavrides E, Sheikh L, et al.
articles.population: Pregnant women and postpartum women
articles.outcomes: Effectiveness of different uterotonic drugs and combinations for preventing postpartum haemorrhage; side-effects of different prophylaxis strategies
articles.take_home: Oxytocin or carbetocin given alone are the safest and most effective drugs for preventing postpartum haemorrhage, though combinations with other drugs can be considered for high-risk women. Prevention also requires good antenatal care, treating anaemia, managing other risk factors, and ensuring births happen in well-resourced hospitals.

articles.title: Reliability and clinical utility of paediatric ankle fracture classifications : a systematic review.
articles.authors: Teo LS, Xiao L, Yeo BKK, Rayapati R, Lim AKS, Hoipo Hui J, et al.
articles.study_type: Systematic review of reliability and clinical utility studies
articles.population: Paediatric ankle fractures
articles.outcomes: Interobserver and intraobserver reliability of classification systems; predictive value for premature physeal closure and angular deformity
articles.take_home: The Dias-Tachdjian classification system was most reliable for doctors to use consistently when classifying children's ankle fractures, whilst the Salter-Harris system was most commonly used and best at predicting whether the growth plate would close prematurely. Using both types of classification together may help doctors predict outcomes and plan treatment better.

articles.title: The impact of Chile's multipronged food labelling and advertising law on early childhood excess weight: a cohort difference-in-differences study.
articles.authors: Paraje G, Valdes N, Macaya AV, Corvalan C, Popkin B
articles.population: School children aged 4-6 years in Chile attending public and publicly subsidised schools
articles.sample_size: 321,597 students from cohorts entering school between 2012 and 2015
articles.outcomes: Probability of excess weight (overweight or obesity) measured by BMI
articles.take_home: Chile's 2016 food labelling and advertising law, which included black warning labels and marketing restrictions, reduced the likelihood of excess weight in young schoolchildren by about 2-3%, with larger effects seen after longer exposure. The law appears to have successfully reduced childhood obesity through changes to the food environment.

articles.title: Low concentration atropine eye drops and progression of myopia in children: multicentre placebo controlled, double masked, randomised trial in the UK (CHAMP-UK).
articles.authors: Azuara-Blanco A, Logan NS, McConnell E, Kearney S, Kirk G, Jones S, et al.
articles.population: Children aged 6-12 years with myopia between -0.50 and -10.0 dioptres
articles.sample_size: 289 children (192 atropine, 97 placebo); 230 with primary outcome data
articles.outcomes: Primary: spherical equivalent refractive error after two years. Secondary: axial length, visual acuity, reading speed, pupil diameter, adverse events, quality of life, tolerability
articles.take_home: Low concentration atropine eye drops (0.01%) significantly slowed myopia progression in children compared with placebo, reducing progression by about 0.33 dioptres over two years, and were well tolerated with no serious side effects.

articles.title: Exa-cel in Children with Transfusion-Dependent beta-Thalassemia or Sickle Cell Disease.
articles.authors: Frangoul H, de la Fuente J, Chopra Y, Meisel R, Amrolia PJ, Algeri M, et al.
articles.population: Children aged 5 to 11 years with transfusion-dependent beta-thalassaemia or sickle cell disease
articles.sample_size: 15 children with beta-thalassaemia and 11 children with sickle cell disease
articles.outcomes: Transfusion independence for 12 consecutive months in beta-thalassaemia; freedom from severe vaso-occlusive crises for 12 consecutive months in sickle cell disease
articles.take_home: All children who had been followed for at least 16 months achieved the main treatment goals: those with beta-thalassaemia no longer needed blood transfusions, and those with sickle cell disease had no severe pain crises. However, all children experienced serious side effects during treatment, and one child with beta-thalassaemia died from liver complications related to the conditioning therapy.

articles.title: Interleukin-10 Autoantibodies and HLA-DRB1*01:03 in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
articles.authors: Gharahdaghi N, Yeh PJ, Ceron-Gutierrez L, Griffin H, Gordon H, Jayamanne C, et al.
articles.population: Patients with inflammatory bowel disease and controls without IBD from Oxford and UK IBD BioResource cohorts
articles.outcomes: Presence of neutralising autoantibodies against interleukin-10 and association with HLA-DRB1*01:03 genetic marker
articles.take_home: About 3.5% of patients with inflammatory bowel disease have autoantibodies that neutralise interleukin-10, a protein that normally helps reduce inflammation, and these patients almost always carry a specific genetic marker called HLA-DRB1*01:03. This finding may help identify a subgroup of IBD patients who could benefit from targeted treatments.

articles.title: Advances in supporting development in autistic children and youth.
articles.authors: Penner M, Zwaigenbaum L, Piroddi N, Park S, Minhas RS, Singal D
articles.population: Autistic children and youth
articles.outcomes: Developmental trajectories, adaptive skills, co-occurring conditions, autonomy, community participation, intervention benefits and adverse events
articles.take_home: Research shows that supporting developmental and adaptive skills early in childhood, monitoring for co-occurring conditions, and promoting acceptance and meaningful inclusion are important for autistic children and young people. Interventions can help, but it is important to listen to what autistic people themselves say matters for living a good life.

articles.title: Time is of the Essence! Long-Term Functional, Motor and Sensory Outcomes After Surgical Fixation of Pediatric Medial Epicondyle Fractures: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
articles.authors: Akin AE, Erkan S, Okcu G
articles.population: Children under 18 years old who underwent surgical fixation for medial epicondyle fracture
articles.sample_size: 22 patients with complete long-term follow-up (from 40 identified)
articles.outcomes: Functional scores (QuickDASH, pain), range of motion, motor strength (grip and pinch), sensation, stability, and complications
articles.take_home: Children who have surgery for medial epicondyle fractures have excellent long-term results overall, but delays in having surgery increase the risk of permanent stiffness in the elbow. Early surgery combined with rehabilitation gives the best chance of a good outcome.

articles.title: Complications of Implant Removal of Pediatric Femoral Shaft Fractures.
articles.authors: Aslanturk O, Roberts C, Siddique R, Rowland J, Sheffer B, Kelly DM
articles.population: Paediatric patients who underwent implant removal after surgical treatment of femoral shaft fractures
articles.outcomes: Operative time, estimated blood loss, length of stay, wound complications, infection, hematoma, bony overgrowth, refracture
articles.take_home: Plate removal took longer and caused more bleeding than nail removal, whilst nail removal was associated with more complications including bony overgrowth around the implant. Overall, implant removal after paediatric femur fractures has a low complication rate with very few refractures in the short term.

articles.title: Advances in the management of ADHD in children and adolescents.
articles.authors: Robaey P, Rogers MA, Schachar RJ
articles.study_type: Narrative review of ADHD management evidence (2019-2025)
articles.population: Children and adolescents with ADHD
articles.outcomes: Diagnostic accuracy, response to behavioural interventions, response to lifestyle changes, response to medication, response to cognitive training and neurofeedback
articles.take_home: ADHD is a chronic condition requiring ongoing personalised care. Effective treatment typically combines medication (stimulants as first line, non-stimulants as alternatives) with behavioural approaches like parent training and school interventions, alongside lifestyle measures such as physical activity and sleep, though individual results vary depending on the person and setting.

articles.title: Factors associated with open reduction of pediatric type III supracondylar humerus fractures.
articles.authors: Najd Mazhar F, Zareie B, Shariatzadeh H, Hasan A, Jafari Kafiabadi M, Akhlaghi N, et al.
articles.population: Children aged 3-13 years with Gartland type III supracondylar humerus fractures
articles.outcomes: Factors associated with need for open reduction versus successful closed reduction and pin fixation
articles.take_home: Older children, those with flexion-type fractures, and those with fractures lower down the bone were more likely to need open surgery rather than closed reduction. Age and fracture location were the strongest predictors of needing open reduction.

articles.title: A Comparison of the Effectiveness of Different Fixation Strategies in Pediatric and Adolescent Subtrochanteric Femur Fractures.
articles.authors: Munshi MA, Siddabattula R, Najera Saltos D, Pereira D, Cornaghie M, Hosseinzadeh P
articles.population: Children and adolescents aged 18 years or younger treated with surgery for subtrochanteric femur fractures
articles.outcomes: Radiographic healing, malalignment (>10 degrees angulation), pain and mobility scores (PROMIS), and complications
articles.take_home: Rigid nails, flexible nails and plate fixation all help paediatric subtrochanteric femur fractures heal and improve function. Although flexible nails had more alignment issues at 6 weeks, all three methods performed similarly by 3 months.

articles.title: Prospective Methodology Results in Higher Rates of Reported Complications Following Pediatric ACL Reconstruction.
articles.authors: Martin H, Ellis H, Agonias K, Van Pelt R, Saper M, Niu EL, et al.
articles.study_type: Prospective multicentre registry study comparing complication rates with non-prospective data
articles.population: Children and young people aged 18 years or under undergoing anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction
articles.sample_size: 8516 cases in the prospective SCORE database (2018-2025)
articles.outcomes: Complication rates within 8 months post-operation including stiffness, reoperation, wound-healing delay, pain, infection, nerve injury, readmission and blood clots
articles.take_home: About 1 in 9 children having ACL reconstruction experienced a complication within 8 months, with stiffness being most common. Prospective data collection identified more complications like pain and stiffness than older non-prospective studies, suggesting better accuracy in tracking what happens after surgery.

articles.title: A Comparison of Patient-Reported and Radiographic Outcomes Across Rigid and Flexible Flatfeet Following Calcaneal Lengthening Osteotomy in Pediatric Patients.
articles.authors: Munshi MA, Siddabattula R, Pereira D, Tang N, Hosseinzadeh P
articles.population: Children aged 8 to 18 years with painful flatfeet (rigid or flexible) undergoing calcaneal lengthening osteotomy
articles.sample_size: 72 children (114 feet) with minimum 6 months follow-up
articles.outcomes: Patient-reported pain and mobility scores (PROMIS); radiographic measurements including talo-first metatarsal angles, talonavicular uncoverage, incongruency angle, calcaneal pitch, and medial cuneiform-5th metatarsal height
articles.take_home: Calcaneal lengthening osteotomy significantly improved pain and radiographic measurements in children with both rigid and flexible flatfeet, with similar outcomes between the two groups. Children experienced meaningful pain relief averaging 7.8 to 9.3 points on pain scores after surgery.

articles.title: Limb occlusion pressure adjusted tourniquet use results in adequate hemostasis in pediatric limb surgeries: a prospective clinical study.
articles.authors: Jain S, Gupta S, Raghav L, Kumar N, Kumar Y, Kalia P
articles.population: Children aged 1-15 years undergoing upper or lower limb surgery
articles.outcomes: Adequate hemostasis achieved in 95.55% of procedures; no tourniquet-related complications observed
articles.take_home: Using a handheld Doppler to measure limb occlusion pressure and set tourniquet pressure accordingly was safe and effective in children's limb surgery, achieving good blood control without complications and using lower pressures than typically needed in adults.

articles.title: Surgical pinning after loss of reduction in Salter-Harris type I-II distal radius fractures does not improve outcomes: A propensity matched cohort study.
articles.authors: Oral M, Kolac UC, Aydinoglu HS, Ibik S, Morkan M, Sili MV, et al.
articles.population: Children under 16 years with Salter-Harris type I or II distal radius fractures who experienced loss of reduction after initial closed reduction
articles.outcomes: Mayo wrist score, DASH score, PRWE score, and radiographic parameters (radial height, dorsal angulation, dorsal translation, radial inclination, physeal alignment) at mean follow-up of 61 months
articles.take_home: Children with these fractures who lost reduction had similar functional and radiographic outcomes whether treated with continued conservative care or surgical pinning, suggesting surgery may not be necessary routinely in this group.
